nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## YogiAI

This YogiAI is to provide daily reminder and pose of the day to the user via Line Push Message

The data will be generated from GoogleSheet Weighted Random Poses and Push to your Line at the scheduled time

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
## Get the Data
This is to get the data from GoogleSheet 

In the range we got, we'll have PosesName, uri (image link), and url (link when clicked) 

The sample is here 
{{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

The data is from {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

***YOU SHOULD UPDATE IT WITH YOUR OWN DATA***

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
## Write FlexMessage for Images

To send the information in Line, we need to write a JSON for Flex Message meaning that it can slides to show the images of the pose

We use auto-parser here to make sure the JSON followed the required format

{{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}

You can also use {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} to simulate the format

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
## Combine the result and push it via Line

1) We used 'Edit Field' to combine all the output
(Hint: you can have input_txt and output_txt to debug your script here)
2) To make sure that our JSON is proper, we asked AI to fix it again. 
3) Use Line Push >> Please replace "to" to your own UID and create the header authorization with the channel you have

{{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note5

parameters.content:
## Write back the data into Log and Log2 

We used log2 to count how many time we send each poses and weighted this back into the 'Yoga' Sheet to make the random more random ;)

To put the data back, we also want to extract from the output and split it out to put back to google sheet

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.text:
=You'll change this into properly format of JSON without having emoji. You'll also make sure the name is matched the data in googlesheet

 {{ $json.Text }}

messageValues.message: =You are JSON formatter, You'll fix the JSON and return only the JSON that has been fixed. Do not explain or write anything else

=You'll format and add emoji before the poses name to make it chat-friendly to send via Line. You will only return the message to be sent.

If the message is too long, you'll split by ====== to 3 messages

parameters.content:
## YogiAI

This YogiAI is to provide daily reminder and pose of the day to the user via Line Push Message

The data will be generated from GoogleSheet Weighted Random Poses and Push to your Line at the scheduled time

## Get the Data
This is to get the data from GoogleSheet 

In the range we got, we'll have PosesName, uri (image link), and url (link when clicked) 

The sample is here 
{{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

The data is from {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

***YOU SHOULD UPDATE IT WITH YOUR OWN DATA***

## Write FlexMessage for Images

To send the information in Line, we need to write a JSON for Flex Message meaning that it can slides to show the images of the pose

We use auto-parser here to make sure the JSON followed the required format

{{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}

You can also use {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} to simulate the format

## Write Text for Poses today 

This node we want to have user friendly text such as with emojis, etc. So, we give Azure OpenAI the poses of today and ask it to rewrie

## Combine the result and push it via Line

1) We used 'Edit Field' to combine all the output
(Hint: you can have input_txt and output_txt to debug your script here)
2) To make sure that our JSON is proper, we asked AI to fix it again. 
3) Use Line Push >> Please replace "to" to your own UID and create the header authorization with the channel you have

{{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}

## Write back the data into Log and Log2 

We used log2 to count how many time we send each poses and weighted this back into the 'Yoga' Sheet to make the random more random ;)

To put the data back, we also want to extract from the output and split it out to put back to google sheet

messageValues.message: =You're experienced yoga instructor. You'll say the topic and asking the student to focus on practice today. You'll later give the yoga poses list for practicing today. You will also include related pose or variation from the list. You'll make sure to include all the poses from the list.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.

Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.

testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
